document.source_title: NEET paper leak: CBI now arrests 2 in Maharashtra; 5 others sent to 7-day custody

claims.claim_text: The CBI has arrested two more accused in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case.
claims.evidence_text: The fresh arrests bring the total number to seven since the agency registered the case on May 12.

entities.name: Central Bureau of Investigation
entities.description: The agency conducting the investigation into the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak.
entities.evidence_text: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has intensified its crackdown in the investigation into the alleged NEET–UG 2026 paper leak.

entities.name: Maharashtra
entities.description: The state in India where two of the accused were arrested.
entities.evidence_text: Special teams of the CBI arrested Dhananjay Lokhanda from Ahilyanagar and Manisha Waghmare from Pune in Maharashtra.

entities.name: NEET-UG 2026
entities.description: The medical entrance exam under scrutiny for alleged paper leaks.
entities.evidence_text: The NEET–UG 2026 exam, conducted earlier this month for over 20 lakh medical aspirants, has been under scrutiny after reports of question paper leaks surfaced.

events.event: CBI registered the case regarding the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak.
events.evidence_text: The fresh arrests bring the total number to seven since the agency registered the case on May 12.

events.event: CBI arrests two more accused in Maharashtra.
events.evidence_text: Special teams of the CBI arrested Dhananjay Lokhanda from Ahilyanagar and Manisha Waghmare from Pune in Maharashtra on Thursday.

metrics.name: Total arrests
metrics.evidence_text: The fresh arrests bring the total number to seven since the agency registered the case on May 12.

metrics.name: Medical aspirants
metrics.evidence_text: The NEET–UG 2026 exam, conducted earlier this month for over 20 lakh medical aspirants.

citations.title: NEET paper leak: CBI now arrests 2 in Maharashtra; 5 others sent to 7-day custody

source_credibility.reason: The source is a reputable news outlet providing detailed information on a significant legal investigation.
